title: Add sections in Forms
excerpt: It would great to be able to create sections in Forms using Headings that are not part of a question. This feature would visually break up the form in a manner that would improve the UX.

posts.author: RandyGarmon (Randy Garmon)
posts.cookedHtml: It would great to be able to create sections in Forms using Headings that are not part of a question. This feature would visually break up the form in a manner that would improve the UX.

posts.author: jtho-seif (Jérémie)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all,

I totally agree with Randy, it would be nice to have this feature

Jérémie

posts.author: Bill_In_Ohio (Bill Duncan)
posts.cookedHtml:
I know this is an older post, but when searching for something else (updating existing records with a form) this idea suggestion came up. I’m not sure if it exists elsewhere - but having section headers on forms are a very standard practice.

We need the ability to present information that doesn’t have a field attached to it. I had to vote for this idea.

posts.author: Dly215 (Duong )
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed with this! Bumping.

posts.author: sabrescia (Sarah Brescia)
posts.cookedHtml: PLEASE read this thread, @monday.com! This is a critical feature.

posts.author: adinap (Adina Pierce)
posts.cookedHtml: This feature can’t come soon enough. Like others said, it’s standard in all other platforms. (Workfront, Jira, Survey Monkey, Google Forms, etc.). Add-ons or integrations are not an option for us.

posts.author: cvdw (Cait VanDeWiele)
posts.cookedHtml: Chiming in, I agree, this feature can’t come soon enough!

posts.author: Kristi (Kristi Fuxan)
posts.cookedHtml: Came here looking for this exact function! It can’t be that hard, can it?

posts.author: Steven1 (Steven)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey! We use forms various purposes. Some forms are pretty long, so it’d be awesome if we could break them down into sections. Imagine having titles or text bits that aren’t questions—just to organize things better. It’d make navigating through, say, a survey a whole lot clearer. Maybe even a feature like the current ‘Click to start,’ but for a ‘Survey Part 2 > Click to continue’?

Thankyou!

posts.author: jramoschapman (Jamilya Ramos-Chapman)
posts.cookedHtml: Here in Jan 2024 to say this feature is still desired!

posts.author: JoshGadow (Josh Gadow)
posts.cookedHtml: This seems like such a simple feature to add. Would convince me to move over from formstack!

posts.author: KL22 (KL)
posts.cookedHtml: Please add this feature! As others have said, downloading an app/add-on to do this is not an option at my org so I need this to be a native feature. I’m using forms for data collection surveys, and being able to break the form into sections (either with headings, or even with a “next section” button) would help make it less tiring on the recipient.

posts.author: mhelwer (Morgan Helwer)
posts.cookedHtml: Agree, I really need this feature.

posts.author: CelesteCav (Celeste)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes please add it. Its standard on other forms.

posts.author: einavpeleg (Einav Peleg)
posts.cookedHtml: This is such a simple and standard practice feature. Please add this to your roadmap🙏

posts.author: kateemily (Kate Biernat)
posts.cookedHtml: Here to add that this is a feature we would also like to have included. Please add this to your pipeline!

posts.author: grace.akinyemi (MamaEND)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello, please are you able to add the alignment features to the start page of the workforms.
If possible allow us able to design it and to be able to break the questions into section - like page break. Thanks.

posts.author: amiraenergyedc (Amira R)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes please! This would be super helpful and should be standard

posts.author: kmcdaniel (Katie McDaniel)
posts.cookedHtml: Honestly surprised this doesn’t exist.

posts.author: Dinah (Dinah Williams)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding to this thread as we use forms a lot and would be a better user experience

posts.author: ChristinaH (Christina)
posts.cookedHtml: Here to ask for this as well.

posts.author: kgundersen (Kristen Gundersen)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1. I just created a form where section headers would have been incredibly helpful, and I’m surprised this feature isn’t available yet.

In the meantime, I’ve created labels on my board as placeholders for section headers and set up an automation to name them according to what I would have used as headers on the form. While it’s not ideal, it helps me and my team stay organized when reviewing form submissions.

posts.author: Chris83 (Chris White)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be very beneficial to our use of forms.

posts.author: Clarey (MondayMotivator)
posts.cookedHtml: I have also voted would love this to be a feature having sections!

posts.author: MauraAckerman (Maura Ackerman)
posts.cookedHtml: This feature is so necessary!

posts.author: amullins (Abby Mullins)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be a huge improvement! Please add!

posts.author: shirozayas (Shiro Zayas)
posts.cookedHtml: Voted for this feature as well. Forms could be complicated sometimes and having sections could definetly improve the user experience.

posts.author: DavidSchenkler (David Schenkler | monday ambassador cohort 7)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’d love for this feature to show up! It’s constantly asked by my team. We use forms constantly, and the need for sections and headings is vital.

Additionally, if there was then a way to flexibly paginate the sections. That’d also be useful.

Finally, similar to Google Form’s section-based answers, having the ability to select which section will be asked/answered based on a question would be helpful. Although there are condition-based questions already in workforms, it is sometimes very frustrating and difficult to add or move questions. Additionally, we sometimes have to duplicate certain questions depending on certain answers.

posts.author: amybronstein (Amy Bronstein)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone, Amy here from the monday.com team! Thank you so much for your feedback around adding sections in forms. We know how important this feature is for organizing and streamlining form responses.

Right now, this feature is under active consideration. As part of our broader efforts to improve the forms experience, we’re currently rebuilding parts of our infrastructure and layout to support the ability to introduce sections in the future. This foundational work is a big step toward making this possible!

We’ll continue to keep you posted as things progress!

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Community! I’m happy to announce that his feature is now on our long-term roadmap. While we don’t have a timeline for release to share yet, we’re making steps towards making this possible! As we move into development, we’ll share more with you in this thread.

posts.author: MeiraPom (Meira Pomegranate)
posts.cookedHtml: 10.2025- This thread is marked as Resolved - but there is still no feature to break a Form into different Sections.. Is the “Solution”/resolution just that it has been added to the long-term list of things to do?

posts.author: TGainey (Tabatha Gainey)
posts.cookedHtml: Do you have a screenshot that you would be willing to share?

posts.author: MickaylaM (Mickayla Murray)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Everyone!

We’re excited to announce that this feature has officially been released! You can now add headings to your forms to create clear sections and improve the overall experience, no workaround required. Just a smoother, more organized form flow. We would love to hear how it feels in your next form build!
